items.source_title: A₁: A VLA with Adaptive Early Exit and Layered Truncated Flow Matching (arXiv 2604.05672)
items.assessment: The arXiv paper confirms the model name, the joint SYSU + MBZUAI authorship, and a 2026 release timeline. This is a pure factual event claim and does not update any current hypothesis.

items.source_title: A₁: A VLA with Adaptive Early Exit and Layered Truncated Flow Matching (arXiv 2604.05672)
items.assessment: The paper's release page lists code, weights, and training data as openly available. No hypothesis attachment: 'fully open source' is an orthogonal fact about release practice, not direct evidence for any current hypothesis about model architecture trends.

items.source_title: A₁: A VLA with Adaptive Early Exit and Layered Truncated Flow Matching (arXiv 2604.05672)
items.assessment: Adaptive per-token early exit — training all VLM layers with a shared action head, then monitoring layer-to-layer action-delta at inference to halt forward propagation — is a textbook realization of 'token efficiency as architecture'. Attached with SLIGHT evidence strength (not moderate) because the modality is VLA/action tokens rather than the LLM text-generation context where this hypothesis originated. Trust = STRONG because it's an arXiv primary source with full open-source release.

items.source_title: A₁: A VLA with Adaptive Early Exit and Layered Truncated Flow Matching (arXiv 2604.05672)
items.assessment: Layered truncated flow matching reduces denoising steps, not tokens. That is a sibling efficiency axis (sampling-step efficiency), not 'token efficiency'. Attaching it to token_efficiency_as_architecture would silently rewrite that hypothesis's scope, so we keep it as a pure technique fact without hypothesis attachment for this round. Revisit if 2–3 more VLA papers expose the same pattern — that would justify a new hypothesis.

items.source_title: A₁: A VLA with Adaptive Early Exit and Layered Truncated Flow Matching (arXiv 2604.05672)
items.assessment: The 72% latency and 76.6% FLOPs reduction numbers, and the 29.00% RoboChallenge score, are all confirmed in the paper's main result tables. These are DOWNSTREAM consequences of both the early-exit mechanism and the truncated flow matching mechanism jointly. Attaching the cost reduction to token_efficiency_as_architecture on top of the early-exit mechanism claim would double-count the same evidence, so this claim is recorded as fact only.
